rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (verifying reduce_scatter in the test alongside device_communicator). X pads its review with five 'minor' findings that are largely stylistic nits (missing docstrings, add-a-comment suggestions, a redundant hasattr assert) presented as issues, inflating actionable-looking line items without much real signal, and its 'COMMENT' verdict sits oddly against content it itself calls all-minor. Y is more concise but demonstrates deeper correctness verification (DPMetadata's actual required fields, why removing the cfg_parallel_size>1 guard is safe via the world_size==1 early-return, destroy_model_parallel cleanup correctness) and reaches an APPROVE verdict consistent with the real reviewers' LGTM outcome.
